title: CIRCT doesn't compile with gcc 7.5.0
body:
The current CIRCT main branch (a470701a49c938b8c08dfa9847234881f9595384) doesn't compile using gcc 7.5.0 (default on ubuntu 18.04).

```
(.venv) smburns@smburns-XPS-13-9370:~/circt/circt/build$ ninja -j16
[19/26] Building CXX object lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/CMakeFiles/obj.CIRCTSVTransforms.dir/SVExtractTestCode.cpp.o
FAILED: lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/CMakeFiles/obj.CIRCTSVTransforms.dir/SVExtractTestCode.cpp.o 
/usr/bin/c++ -DGTEST_HAS_RTTI=0 -D_DEBUG -D__STDC_CONSTANT_MACROS -D__STDC_FORMAT_MACROS -D__STDC_LIMIT_MACROS -I/home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/llvm/include -I/home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/build/include -I/home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/mlir/include -I/home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/build/tools/mlir/include -I/home/smburns/circt/circt/include -I/home/smburns/circt/circt/build/include -fno-exceptions -fno-rtti -fPIC -fno-semantic-interposition -fvisibility-inlines-hidden -Werror=date-time -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter -Wwrite-strings -Wcast-qual -Wno-missing-field-initializers -Wimplicit-fallthrough -Wno-noexcept-type -Wdelete-non-virtual-dtor -Wno-comment -Wmisleading-indentation -fdiagnostics-color -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -O3 -DNDEBUG  -fno-exceptions -fno-rtti -UNDEBUG -std=gnu++14 -MD -MT lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/CMakeFiles/obj.CIRCTSVTransforms.dir/SVExtractTestCode.cpp.o -MF lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/CMakeFiles/obj.CIRCTSVTransforms.dir/SVExtractTestCode.cpp.o.d -o lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/CMakeFiles/obj.CIRCTSVTransforms.dir/SVExtractTestCode.cpp.o -c /home/smburns/circt/circt/lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/SVExtractTestCode.cpp
/home/smburns/circt/circt/lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/SVExtractTestCode.cpp: In function ‘void getBackwardSliceSimple(mlir::Operation*, llvm::SmallPtrSetImpl<mlir::Operation*>&, std::function<bool(mlir::Operation*)>)’:
/home/smburns/circt/circt/lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/SVExtractTestCode.cpp:44:20: error: parse error in template argument list
     if (!op || op->hasTrait<OpTrait::IsIsolatedFromAbove>())
                    ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/home/smburns/circt/circt/lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/SVExtractTestCode.cpp:44:59: error: no matching function for call to ‘mlir::Operation::hasTrait<<expression error> >()’
     if (!op || op->hasTrait<OpTrait::IsIsolatedFromAbove>())
                                                           ^
In file included from /home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/mlir/include/mlir/IR/OpDefinition.h:23:0,
                 from /home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/mlir/include/mlir/IR/FunctionSupport.h:18,
                 from /home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/mlir/include/mlir/IR/BuiltinOps.h:16,
                 from /home/smburns/circt/circt/include/circt/Dialect/SV/SVDialect.h:18,
                 from /home/smburns/circt/circt/include/circt/Dialect/SV/SVOps.h:16,
                 from /home/smburns/circt/circt/lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/PassDetail.h:15,
                 from /home/smburns/circt/circt/lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/SVExtractTestCode.cpp:16:
/home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/mlir/include/mlir/IR/Operation.h:476:53: note: candidate: template<template<class T> class Trait> bool mlir::Operation::hasTrait()
   template <template <typename T> class Trait> bool hasTrait() {
                                                     ^~~~~~~~
/home/smburns/circt/circt/llvm/mlir/include/mlir/IR/Operation.h:476:53: note:   template argument deduction/substitution failed:
/home/smburns/circt/circt/lib/Dialect/SV/Transforms/SVExtractTestCode.cpp:44:59: error: template argument 1 is invalid
     if (!op || op->hasTrait<OpTrait::IsIsolatedFromAbove>())
                                                           ^
[20/26] Linking CXX executable bin/llhd-sim
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.
```

This is the command:
```
cmake -G Ninja ..     -DMLIR_DIR=$PWD/../llvm/build/lib/cmake/mlir     -DLLVM_DIR=$PWD/../llvm/build/lib/cmake/llvm     -DLLVM_ENABLE_ASSERTIONS=ON     -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release     -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON
ninja -j16
```

I do have success if I use the 6.0.0 version of clang.
```
CC=clang CXX=clang++ cmake -G Ninja ..     -DMLIR_DIR=$PWD/../llvm/build/lib/cmake/mlir     -DLLVM_DIR=$PWD/../llvm/build/lib/cmake/llvm     -DLLVM_ENABLE_ASSERTIONS=ON     -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release     -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON
```

Should I be using clang? Or a different version of GCC?
